server.name: jacs-mcp
server.title: JACS MCP Server
server.instructions: This MCP server provides data provenance and cryptographic signing for agent state files and agent-to-agent messaging. Agent state tools: jacs_sign_state (sign files), jacs_verify_state (verify integrity), jacs_load_state (load with verification), jacs_update_state (update and re-sign), jacs_list_state (list signed docs), jacs_adopt_state (adopt external files). Memory tools: jacs_memory_save (save a memory), jacs_memory_recall (search memories by query), jacs_memory_list (list all memories), jacs_memory_forget (soft-delete a memory), jacs_memory_update (update an existing memory). Messaging tools: jacs_message_send (create and sign a message), jacs_message_update (update and re-sign a message), jacs_message_agree (co-sign/agree to a message), jacs_message_receive (verify and extract a received message). Agent management: jacs_create_agent (create new agent with keys), jacs_reencrypt_key (rotate private key password). A2A artifacts: jacs_wrap_a2a_artifact (sign artifact with provenance), jacs_verify_a2a_artifact (verify wrapped artifact), jacs_assess_a2a_agent (assess remote agent trust level). A2A discovery: jacs_export_agent_card (export Agent Card), jacs_generate_well_known (generate .well-known documents), jacs_export_agent (export full agent JSON). Trust store: jacs_trust_agent (add agent to trust store), jacs_untrust_agent (remove from trust store, requires JACS_MCP_ALLOW_UNTRUST=true), jacs_list_trusted_agents (list all trusted agent IDs), jacs_is_trusted (check if agent is trusted), jacs_get_trusted_agent (get trusted agent JSON). Attestation: jacs_attest_create (create signed attestation with claims), jacs_attest_verify (verify attestation, optionally with evidence checks), jacs_attest_lift (lift signed document into attestation), jacs_attest_export_dsse (export attestation as DSSE envelope). Security: jacs_audit (read-only security audit and health checks). Audit trail: jacs_audit_log (record events as signed audit entries), jacs_audit_query (search audit trail by action, target, time range), jacs_audit_export (export audit trail as signed bundle). Search: jacs_search (unified search across all signed documents).

tools.name: jacs_adopt_state
tools.description: Adopt an external file as signed agent state. Like sign_state but marks the origin as 'adopted' and optionally records the source URL.

input_schema.description: Parameters for adopting an external agent state file.

description.description: Optional description of what this adopted state document contains

file_path.description: Path to the file to adopt and sign as agent state

name.description: Human-readable name for this adopted state document

source_url.description: Optional URL where the content was originally obtained

state_type.description: Type of agent state: memory, skill, plan, config, or hook

input_schema.title: AdoptStateParams

tools.name: jacs_assess_a2a_agent
tools.description: Assess the trust level of a remote A2A agent given its Agent Card. Applies a trust policy (open, verified, or strict) and returns whether the agent is allowed and at what trust level.

input_schema.description: Parameters for assessing trust level of a remote A2A agent.

agent_card_json.description: The A2A Agent Card JSON of the remote agent to assess

policy.description: Trust policy: 'open' (accept all), 'verified' (require JACS), or 'strict' (require trust store)

input_schema.title: AssessA2aAgentParams

tools.name: jacs_attest_create
tools.description: Create a signed attestation document. Provide a JSON string with: subject (type, id, digests), claims (name, value, confidence, assuranceLevel), and optional evidence, derivation, and policyContext. Requires the attestation feature.

input_schema.description: Parameters for creating an attestation.

params_json.description: JSON string containing attestation parameters: { subject: { type, id, digests }, claims: [{ name, value, confidence?, assuranceLevel? }], evidence?: [...], derivation?: {...}, policyContext?: {...} }

input_schema.title: AttestCreateParams

tools.name: jacs_attest_export_dsse
tools.description: Export an attestation as a DSSE envelope for in-toto/SLSA compatibility. Provide the signed attestation document JSON. Returns a DSSE envelope with payloadType, payload, and signatures. Requires the attestation feature.

input_schema.description: Parameters for exporting an attestation as a DSSE envelope.

attestation_json.description: JSON string of the signed attestation document to export as DSSE

input_schema.title: AttestExportDsseParams

tools.name: jacs_attest_lift
tools.description: Lift an existing signed JACS document into an attestation. Provide the signed document JSON and a JSON array of claims to attach. Requires the attestation feature.

input_schema.description: Parameters for lifting a signed document to an attestation.

claims_json.description: JSON array of claim objects: [{ name, value, confidence?, assuranceLevel? }]

signed_doc_json.description: JSON string of the existing signed JACS document to lift

input_schema.title: AttestLiftParams

tools.name: jacs_attest_verify
tools.description: Verify an attestation document. Provide a document_key in 'jacsId:jacsVersion' format. Set full=true for full-tier verification including evidence and derivation chain checks. Requires the attestation feature.

input_schema.description: Parameters for verifying an attestation.

full.description: Set to true for full-tier verification (evidence + chain checks)

input_schema.title: AttestVerifyParams

tools.name: jacs_audit
tools.description: Run a read-only JACS security audit and health checks. Returns a JSON report with risks, health_checks, summary, and overall_status. Does not modify state. Optional: config_path, recent_n (number of recent documents to re-verify).

input_schema.description: Parameters for the JACS security audit tool.

recent_n.description: Number of recent documents to re-verify (default from config)

input_schema.title: JacsAuditParams

tools.name: jacs_audit_export
tools.description: Export audit trail entries for a time period as a single signed JACS document. The exported bundle contains all matching audit entries and can be verified independently.

input_schema.description: Parameters for exporting the audit trail as a signed bundle.

end_time.description: ISO 8601 end time for the export range (required)

start_time.description: ISO 8601 start time for the export range (required)

input_schema.title: AuditExportParams

tools.name: jacs_audit_log
tools.description: Record a tool-use, data-access, or other event as a cryptographically signed audit trail entry. The entry is stored as a private agentstate document with type 'hook'. Use this to maintain a tamper-evident log of agent actions.

input_schema.description: Parameters for recording an audit trail event.

action.description: Action type for this audit entry (e.g., 'tool_use', 'data_access', 'sign', 'verify')

details.description: Additional JSON-formatted details about the event

tags.description: Optional tags for categorizing this audit entry

target.description: What was acted upon (e.g., a JACS document ID or resource identifier)

input_schema.title: AuditLogParams

tools.name: jacs_audit_query
tools.description: Search the audit trail by action type, target, and/or time range. Returns matching audit entries from the signed audit log. Supports pagination.

input_schema.description: Parameters for querying the audit trail.

end_time.description: ISO 8601 end time for the query range (e.g., '2025-12-31T23:59:59Z')

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 50)

start_time.description: ISO 8601 start time for the query range (e.g., '2025-01-01T00:00:00Z')

input_schema.title: AuditQueryParams

tools.name: jacs_check_agreement
tools.description: Check the status of an agreement: how many agents have signed, whether quorum is met, whether it has expired, and which agents still need to sign. Use this to decide whether an agreement is complete and ready to act on.

input_schema.description: Parameters for checking agreement status.

signed_agreement.description: The agreement JSON to check status of

input_schema.title: CheckAgreementParams

tools.name: jacs_create_agent
tools.description: Create a new JACS agent with cryptographic keys. This is the programmatic equivalent of 'jacs create'. Returns agent ID and key paths. SECURITY: Requires JACS_MCP_ALLOW_REGISTRATION=true environment variable.

input_schema.description: Parameters for creating a new JACS agent programmatically.

password.description: Password for encrypting the private key. Must be at least 8 characters with uppercase, lowercase, digit, and special character.

input_schema.title: CreateAgentProgrammaticParams

tools.name: jacs_create_agreement
tools.description: Create a multi-party cryptographic agreement. Use this when multiple agents need to formally agree on something -- like approving a deployment, authorizing a data transfer, or ratifying a decision. You specify which agents must sign, an optional quorum (e.g., 2-of-3), a timeout deadline, and algorithm constraints. Returns a signed agreement document to pass to other agents for co-signing.

input_schema.description: Parameters for creating a multi-party agreement.

agent_ids.description: List of agent IDs (UUIDs) that are parties to this agreement

document.description: JSON document that all parties will agree to. Can be any valid JSON object.

question.description: Question for signers, e.g. 'Do you approve deploying model v2?'

quorum.description: Minimum signatures required (M-of-N). If omitted, all agents must sign.

required_algorithms.description: Only allow these signing algorithms. Values: 'RSA-PSS', 'ring-Ed25519', 'pq2025'

timeout.description: ISO 8601 deadline after which the agreement expires. Example: '2025-12-31T23:59:59Z'

input_schema.title: CreateAgreementParams

tools.name: jacs_export_agent
tools.description: Export the local agent's full JACS JSON document. This includes the agent's identity, public key hash, and signed metadata.

input_schema.description: Parameters for exporting the local agent's full JSON document (no params needed).
input_schema.title: ExportAgentParams

tools.name: jacs_export_agent_card
tools.description: Export this agent's A2A Agent Card as JSON. The Agent Card describes the agent's capabilities, endpoints, and identity for A2A discovery.

input_schema.description: Parameters for exporting the local agent's A2A Agent Card (no params needed).
input_schema.title: ExportAgentCardParams

tools.name: jacs_generate_well_known
tools.description: Generate all .well-known documents for A2A discovery. Returns an array of {path, document} objects that can be served at each path for agent discovery.

input_schema.description: Parameters for generating well-known documents.
input_schema.title: GenerateWellKnownParams

tools.name: jacs_get_trusted_agent
tools.description: Retrieve the full agent JSON document for a trusted agent from the local trust store. Fails if the agent is not trusted.

input_schema.description: Parameters for getting a trusted agent's details.

agent_id.description: The JACS agent ID (UUID format) to retrieve from the trust store

input_schema.title: GetTrustedAgentParams

tools.name: jacs_is_trusted
tools.description: Check whether a specific agent is in the local trust store. Returns a boolean indicating trust status.

input_schema.description: Parameters for checking if an agent is trusted.

agent_id.description: The JACS agent ID (UUID format) to check trust status for

input_schema.title: IsTrustedParams

tools.name: jacs_list_state
tools.description: List signed agent state documents, with optional filtering by type, framework, or tags.

input_schema.description: Parameters for listing signed agent state documents.

state_type.description: Filter by state type: memory, skill, plan, config, or hook

tags.description: Filter by tags (documents must have all specified tags)

input_schema.title: ListStateParams

tools.name: jacs_list_trusted_agents
tools.description: List all agent IDs currently in the local trust store. Returns the count and a list of trusted agent IDs.

input_schema.description: Parameters for listing trusted agents (no parameters required).
input_schema.title: ListTrustedAgentsParams

tools.name: jacs_load_state
tools.description: Load a signed agent state document and optionally verify it before returning the content.

input_schema.description: Parameters for loading a signed agent state.

file_path.description: DEPRECATED for MCP security: direct file-path loading is disabled. Use jacs_id.

require_verified.description: Whether to require verification before loading (default true)

input_schema.title: LoadStateParams

tools.name: jacs_memory_forget
tools.description: Mark a memory document as removed. The document's provenance chain is preserved but the memory is no longer returned by recall or list operations.

input_schema.description: Parameters for forgetting (soft-deleting) a memory.

jacs_id.description: JACS document ID of the memory to forget (uuid:version)

input_schema.title: MemoryForgetParams

tools.name: jacs_memory_list
tools.description: List all saved memory documents with optional filtering by tags or framework. Supports pagination via limit and offset parameters.

input_schema.description: Parameters for listing memories with optional filtering.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 20)

tags.description: Optional tag filter (memories must have all specified tags)

input_schema.title: MemoryListParams

tools.name: jacs_memory_recall
tools.description: Search saved memories by query string and optional tag filter. Returns matching private memory documents. Use this to retrieve previously saved context or information.

input_schema.description: Parameters for recalling (searching) memories.

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 10)

query.description: Search query to match against memory name, content, and description

tags.description: Optional tag filter (memories must have all specified tags)

input_schema.title: MemoryRecallParams

tools.name: jacs_memory_save
tools.description: Save a memory as a cryptographically signed private document. The memory is stored as an agentstate document with type 'memory' and private visibility. Use this to persist context, decisions, or learned information across sessions.

input_schema.description: Parameters for saving a memory.

content.description: Memory content to save (text, JSON, markdown, etc.)

description.description: Optional description of what this memory contains

tags.description: Optional tags for categorization and filtering

input_schema.title: MemorySaveParams

tools.name: jacs_memory_update
tools.description: Update an existing memory with new content, name, or tags. Creates a new signed version linked to the previous version.

input_schema.description: Parameters for updating an existing memory.

content.description: New content for the memory (replaces existing content)

jacs_id.description: JACS document ID of the memory to update (uuid:version)

name.description: New name for the memory (replaces existing name)

tags.description: New tags for the memory (replaces all existing tags)

input_schema.title: MemoryUpdateParams

tools.name: jacs_message_agree
tools.description: Verify and co-sign (agree to) a received signed message. Creates an agreement document that references the original message.

input_schema.description: Parameters for agreeing to (co-signing) a received message.

signed_message.description: The full signed JSON document to agree to

input_schema.title: MessageAgreeParams

tools.name: jacs_message_receive
tools.description: Verify a received signed message and extract its content, sender ID, and timestamp. Use this to validate authenticity before processing a message from another agent.

input_schema.description: Parameters for receiving and verifying a signed message.

signed_message.description: The full signed JSON document received from another agent

input_schema.title: MessageReceiveParams

tools.name: jacs_message_send
tools.description: Create and cryptographically sign a message for sending to another agent. Returns the signed JACS document that can be transmitted to the recipient.

input_schema.description: Parameters for sending a signed message to another agent.

recipient_agent_id.description: The JACS agent ID of the recipient (UUID format)

input_schema.title: MessageSendParams

tools.name: jacs_message_update
tools.description: Update and re-sign an existing message document with new content.

input_schema.description: Parameters for updating an existing signed message.

jacs_id.description: JACS document ID of the message to update

input_schema.title: MessageUpdateParams

tools.name: jacs_reencrypt_key
tools.description: Re-encrypt the agent's private key with a new password. Use this to rotate the password protecting the private key without changing the key itself.

input_schema.description: Parameters for re-encrypting the agent's private key.

new_password.description: New password. Must be at least 8 characters with uppercase, lowercase, digit, and special character.

old_password.description: Current password for the private key

input_schema.title: ReencryptKeyParams

tools.name: jacs_search
tools.description: Search across all signed documents using the unified search interface. Supports fulltext search with optional filtering by document type. Results include relevance scores and matching snippets. The search method (fulltext, vector, or hybrid) is chosen automatically based on the storage backend.

SearchFieldFilter.description: Field-level filter accepted by the MCP search tool.

value.description: Exact value the field must match.

input_schema.description: Parameters for the unified search tool.

agent_id.description: Optional filter by signing agent ID

field_filter.description: Optional JSON field filter for exact matches

jacs_type.description: Optional filter by JACS document type (e.g., 'agentstate', 'message', 'agreement')

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 20)

min_score.description: Optional minimum score threshold between 0.0 and 1.0

query.description: Search query string to match against document content and names

input_schema.title: SearchParams

tools.name: jacs_sign_agreement
tools.description: Co-sign an existing agreement. Use this after receiving an agreement document from another agent. Your cryptographic signature is added to the agreement. The updated document can then be passed to the next signer or checked for completion.

input_schema.description: Parameters for signing an existing agreement.

signed_agreement.description: The full agreement JSON to sign. Obtained from jacs_create_agreement or from another agent.

input_schema.title: SignAgreementParams

tools.name: jacs_sign_document
tools.description: Sign arbitrary JSON content to create a cryptographically signed JACS document. Use this for attestation -- when you want to prove that content was signed by this agent. Returns the signed envelope with hash and document ID.

input_schema.description: Parameters for signing arbitrary content as a JACS document.

content.description: The JSON content to sign as a JACS document

input_schema.title: SignDocumentParams

tools.name: jacs_sign_state
tools.description: Sign an agent state file (memory, skill, plan, config, or hook) to create a cryptographically signed JACS document. This establishes provenance and integrity for the file's contents.

input_schema.description: Parameters for signing an agent state file.

description.description: Optional description of what this state document contains

embed.description: Whether to embed file content inline (default false, always true for hooks)

file_path.description: Path to the file to sign as agent state

name.description: Human-readable name for this state document

state_type.description: Type of agent state: memory, skill, plan, config, or hook

input_schema.title: SignStateParams

tools.name: jacs_trust_agent
tools.description: Add an agent to the local trust store. The agent's self-signature is cryptographically verified before it is trusted. Pass the full agent JSON document. Returns the trusted agent ID on success.

input_schema.description: Parameters for adding an agent to the local trust store.

agent_json.description: The full JACS agent JSON document to add to the trust store

input_schema.title: TrustAgentParams

tools.name: jacs_untrust_agent
tools.description: Remove an agent from the local trust store. SECURITY: Requires JACS_MCP_ALLOW_UNTRUST=true environment variable to prevent prompt injection attacks from removing trusted agents without user consent.

input_schema.description: Parameters for removing an agent from the trust store.

agent_id.description: The JACS agent ID (UUID format) to remove from the trust store

input_schema.title: UntrustAgentParams

tools.name: jacs_update_state
tools.description: Update a previously signed agent state file. Writes new content (if provided), recomputes the SHA-256 hash, and creates a new signed version.

input_schema.description: Parameters for updating a signed agent state.

file_path.description: DEPRECATED for MCP security: direct file-path updates are disabled. Use jacs_id.

new_content.description: New embedded content for the JACS state document. If omitted, re-signs current embedded content.

input_schema.title: UpdateStateParams

tools.name: jacs_verify_a2a_artifact
tools.description: Verify a JACS-wrapped A2A artifact. Checks the cryptographic signature and hash to confirm the artifact was signed by the claimed agent and has not been tampered with.

input_schema.description: Parameters for verifying a JACS-wrapped A2A artifact.

wrapped_artifact.description: The JACS-wrapped A2A artifact JSON to verify

input_schema.title: VerifyA2aArtifactParams

tools.name: jacs_verify_document
tools.description: Verify a signed JACS document given its full JSON string. Checks both the content hash and cryptographic signature. Use this when you have a signed document in memory (e.g. from an approval context or message payload) and need to confirm its integrity and authenticity.

input_schema.description: Parameters for verifying a raw signed JACS document string.

document.description: The full signed JACS document JSON string to verify

input_schema.title: VerifyDocumentParams

tools.name: jacs_verify_state
tools.description: Verify the integrity and authenticity of a signed agent state. Checks both the file hash and the cryptographic signature.

input_schema.description: Parameters for verifying an agent state file or document.

file_path.description: DEPRECATED for MCP security: direct file-path verification is disabled. Use jacs_id.

input_schema.title: VerifyStateParams

tools.name: jacs_wrap_a2a_artifact
tools.description: Wrap an A2A artifact with JACS provenance. Signs the artifact JSON, binding this agent's identity to the content. Optionally include parent signatures for chain-of-custody provenance.

input_schema.description: Parameters for wrapping an A2A artifact with JACS provenance.

artifact_json.description: The A2A artifact JSON content to wrap with JACS provenance

parent_signatures.description: Optional JSON array of parent signatures for chain-of-custody provenance

input_schema.title: WrapA2aArtifactParams
